questions.question: Which example best represents continuous improvement in Information Assurance?

options.B: Regularly updating security controls and procedures
options.D: Allowing all devices into the network

questions.question: What is the purpose of access control policies?

options.B: To determine who can access specific information and resources

questions.question: Which of the following best describes risk mitigation in information assurance?

options.A: Ignoring possible threats to save costs
options.B: Reducing the impact or likelihood of risks

questions.question: Which of the following best explains integrity in information assurance?

options.C: Data is copied to multiple devices

questions.question: Which of the following is a preventive measure in Information Assurance?

options.A: Recovering deleted files after an attack
options.B: Installing antivirus software before threats occur

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an information asset?
Which of the following is an example of a physical security policy?

options.B: Locking server rooms with access cards

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of effective assessment?
Which situation shows a violation of information assurance policy?
Which of the following best describes a vulnerability?

options.B: A weakness that can be exploited by threats

questions.question: Why do organizations restrict user access based on job roles?

options.A: To reduce unnecessary exposure to sensitive information

questions.question: Which of the following best describes criterion-referenced evaluation?

options.B: Students are measured against learning standards
options.C: Students are ranked from highest to lowest

questions.question: Which of the following assets is most likely targeted during a ransomware attack?
Which example best represents a technical countermeasure?
Which of the following is commonly included in an information assurance policy?
What type of policy defines how confidential information should be shared?
Which characteristic ensures that users are truly who they claim to be?
Which of the following is classified as a human-related risk?
Which characteristic of Information Assurance focuses on keeping data accurate and unaltered?
Which assessment method best measures practical skills?
Which of the following shows the availability characteristic of Information Assurance?

options.C: Ensuring systems are accessible when needed

questions.question: Which policy helps employees create strong passwords?
Which action best supports the integrity of information?
Which situation best illustrates a security risk?

options.B: A weak password used for company accounts

questions.question: Which statement best explains diagnostic assessment?

options.B: It identifies students' strengths and weaknesses before instruction
options.C: It is used only for grading

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a summative assessment?
Which statement best explains the relationship between assets and risks?

options.A: Risks exist only when there are valuable assets to protect
options.B: Assets are created to increase risks
options.D: Assets cannot be affected by threats

questions.question: What type of risk can be reduced through multi-factor authentication?
A company regularly updates employee passwords every 90 days. What is the main purpose of this action?
What is the primary goal of creating data backups?

options.C: To recover information after data loss

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the goal of an Information Assurance (IA) process?

options.B: To ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information

questions.question: Which characteristic of Information Assurance ensures that data and systems can be recovered after failures?
Why do organizations conduct regular security training for employees?
Which of the following is the best example of a countermeasure against phishing attacks?
Which of the following best describes an asset in information security?
Which type of assessment is usually conducted during instruction to monitor learning progress?
Which of the following best explains the concept of defense in depth?

options.B: Applying multiple layers of security controls

questions.question: Why is software patching important in risk mitigation?

options.B: It fixes vulnerabilities that attackers may exploit
options.D: It increases the chance of attacks

questions.question: Which situation demonstrates confidentiality?

options.A: A backup server restoring lost data
options.B: A user accessing only authorized files
options.C: A system remaining online during outages

questions.question: Which of the following is a primary goal of information assurance?

options.B: Protecting confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information

questions.question: Which assessment activity best checks understanding during a lesson?
Which of the following is considered a physical asset?
A company installs surge protectors for computers. What risk is being mitigated?
Which action best reduces the risk of phishing attacks?

options.C: Verifying email sources before clicking links

questions.question: Which of the following best describes an information assurance policy?

options.B: A document that defines rules for protecting information assets

questions.question: Why is monitoring network activity important in risk mitigation?

options.A: It helps identify suspicious behavior early

questions.question: Which action best follows an organization's information assurance policy?

options.C: Reporting suspicious emails to IT staff

questions.question: What is the focus of formative assessment?
Which of the following is considered an authentic assessment?
Which of the following best describes evaluation in education?
